summary: Follow-ups and completed automation from the function-budget refactor and post-check finalizer CI artifact task.

observations.problem: The 2026-05-10 follow-up implemented the owner slices that previously accounted for the remaining narrow function-budget proposals: artifact freshness envelope/build-report split, auto-improve session request stabilization, mechanism review/session calibration helpers, mutation proposal assembly helpers, readiness loader/render hardening, observability routing aggregate split, and integration fixture helper extraction.
observations.why_it_matters: The function-budget report now distinguishes actionable narrow proposals from larger coupled candidates instead of encouraging metric-chasing refactors across broad runtime or test surfaces.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep using make function-budget-refactor-proposals as the triage source. Treat proposal_count as the actionable refactor queue and leave non-proposal candidates to owner-specific design work unless they become narrow enough for the proposal policy.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated in the 2026-05-10 runtime refactor closeout: make function-budget-refactor-proposals now reports proposal_count=0 and non_proposal_candidate_count=42. Do not lower thresholds or suppress the remaining non-proposal candidates; revisit them only as part of bounded owner work with focused tests and closeout gates.

observations.problem: Dry-run post-check finalizer diagnostics previously required opening the full JSON report to find recommended_targets.
observations.why_it_matters: When fixed-point closeout fails in CI or release dispatch, operators need the next target list immediately visible as a small artifact instead of scanning a large report payload.
observations.automation_candidate: Materialize recommended_targets as a newline-delimited artifact next to tmp/release-closeout-post-check-finalizer.json and upload both files from CI/release diagnostic steps.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated in this task: make release-closeout-post-check-finalizer-ci-artifact writes the JSON report plus tmp/release-closeout-post-check-finalizer-recommended-targets.txt; CI uploads both and release dispatch exposes the same optional post-check finalizer diagnostics artifact. Keep future dry-run target naming aligned with this pair.

observations.problem: Running public-check with PUBLIC_PYTHON=.venv/bin/python fails after export because the check commands run with cwd set to the public mirror directory, where the repo-local relative virtualenv path does not exist.
observations.why_it_matters: Operators who override PUBLIC_PYTHON with a repo-relative interpreter can get a FileNotFoundError before the public mirror diagnostics report is written, even though the same interpreter works from the source checkout.
observations.automation_candidate: Resolve path-like public_python values against the source vault before invoking commands in the exported public mirror while preserving virtualenv symlinks.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated after the runtime refactor closeout: public_check_summary now resolves PUBLIC_PYTHON=.venv/bin/python to the source checkout absolute virtualenv path before changing cwd to the exported public mirror. Added a regression in tests/test_public_check_summary.py and verified make public-check PUBLIC_PYTHON=.venv/bin/python PYTHON=.venv/bin/python writes a passing ops/reports/public-check-summary.json.
